title: campanula rupestris subsp. celsii
intro: Campanula rupestris subsp. celsii, also known as Alpine Bellflower, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the Apennine Mountains of Italy. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The Alpine Bellflower is believed to offer various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and soothing respiratory problems. The plant is typically prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions to utilize its medicinal properties, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
